meta.title: Change Language | ProxMenux Documentation
meta.description: Switch ProxMenux UI between English, Spanish, French, German, Italian or Portuguese. Translation install only — uses googletrans + a pre-cached translation table. The menu reloads automatically with the new language.
meta.ogDescription: Pick the ProxMenux interface language from the Settings menu. Six languages supported.

header.title: Change Language
header.description: Switch the ProxMenux UI between six supported languages. Saves the choice to config.json and re-execs the menu with the new language. Only available on the Translation install type — the Normal (lightweight) install is English-only by design.

intro.title: What this is
intro.body: A dialog menu that lists the six supported languages. Pick one, ProxMenux saves it to config.json as .language, then re-execs config_menu.sh so the new language takes effect immediately.

warn.title: Translation install only
warn.body: This option only appears in Settings if both checks pass: /opt/googletrans-env/bin/activate exists (Python venv with googletrans), and config.json has a non-empty .language. On the Normal (English-only) install neither is present and the language picker is not shown.

englishTip.title: English is the most accurate
englishTip.body: Non-English strings are produced by a pre-cached table seeded from googletrans. Edge-case strings (rarely triggered options, recent additions) may fall through to a live googletrans call or to the English original. If a translation feels off, English is always the source of truth.

underHood.heading: How it works under the hood
items: Dialog menu lists the 6 codes; you pick one.
If config.json exists: jq --arg lang "$new_language" '.language = $lang' updates the field in place.
If config.json doesn't exist: a fresh one is created with the language code in a single-field object.
exec bash config_menu.sh reloads the Settings menu with the new language active.

troubleshoot.heading: Troubleshooting
troubleshoot.noOptionBody: You're on the Normal (English-only) install. To get language support, reinstall ProxMenux choosing the Translation install option (it pulls Python venv + googletrans).
troubleshoot.stillEnglishTitle: Language changed but UI still shows English
troubleshoot.stillEnglishBody: The menu re-execs itself after the change. If you opened a fresh shell and started a different ProxMenux entrypoint, run menu again to pick up the new language. Confirm: jq -r '.language' /usr/local/share/proxmenux/config.json.

items.label: Show Version Information
items.tail: — confirms install type and current language.
